put.description: Move a connection to another workspace. The target workspace must be in the same environment as the original one.

get.description:
Retrieve all the environments according to filters.

If the user is entitled with CLUSTER_MANAGEMENT or ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role, all environments matching query will be returned.

If the user is entitled with OPERATOR role, environments matching query and the user workspace permissions will be returned.

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

post.description:
Create an Environment. 

Requirements: 
* environment name must be unique
* the user account issuing the request must be granted the ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT permission

Environment is created on platform. 
A CUSTOM workspace is automatically created within this environment with proper ownership permissions assigned onto it. 

Upon your request, Cloud Engine could also be assigned to the newly added environment.

name.description: Unique name for the environment to create

maxCloudContainers.description: Number of Cloud Engines to assign to this Environment

workspaceName.description: Name for the custom workspace created along with the Environment

owner.description: Login name of user to be owner of the custom workspace created along with the Environment

401.description: User has not the right credentials for completing creation

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

put.description:
Update the identified Environment.
Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role.

401.description: User has not the required credentials

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

delete.description:
Remove the identified Environment and all its subparts : workspaces, executables (tasks and plans), etc. 

Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role.

Environment *in use* won't be removed : if any engine is assigned to this environment or if any promotion relates to this environment, removal is not achieved. 

**Please make sure Environment removal is confirmed before calling this endpoint as removal will be cascading all subsidiary elements within the environment : workspaces, tasks, plans, etc. With no cancellation service available. **

400.description:
"To delete the environment, you must remove or re-assign its engine(s) to another environment." or
"Cannot delete environment: it is used in promotions(s)"

401.description: User has not the required credentials

403.description: Environment is still in use, it shall not be removed

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

get.description:
Retrieve all the workspaces according to filters.

If the user is entitled with CLUSTER_MANAGEMENT or ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role, all workspaces matching query will be returned.

If the user is entitled with OPERATOR role, workspaces matching query and the workspace permissions will be returned.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
This parameter defines a search query in FIQL format.
The Feed Item Query Language is a syntax for expressing filters across the entries.

schema.description:
This parameter defines a search query in FIQL format.
The Feed Item Query Language is a syntax for expressing filters across the entries.

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

post.description:
Create a Workspace. 

Please ensure the following requirements are met :
* provide environment this workspace will belong to
* provide some unique naming within environment 
* user must be granted ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT permission

Owner can be provided. By default, creator user would be owner. 
Type is Custom.

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

put.description:
Update the identified Workspace.
Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role.

protectedArtifactUpdate.description:
Allows to restrict the scope of task updates when a new artifact version is published. 
- true : the tasks of this workspace are protected from the unintended updates triggered by artifacts being published in other workspaces. These tasks are updated only when artifacts are being published in the same workspace
- false : tasks that have been set to use the latest version of the published artifact, or any other artifact that has the same name as the newly published artifact, are all updated , whatever workspace is used for this artifact publishing.

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

delete.description:
Remove the Workspace and all its subparts : executables (tasks and plans), permissions, etc. 

Make sure the user is entitled with ENVIRONMENT_MANAGEMENT role. 

Custom Workspaces only can be removed using this API. (Shared and Personal are linked either to Environment or User's life cycle)

Workspace in use won’t be removed : if any task, artifact, resource, connection is used in any other workspace, removal is not achieved. Please make sure Workspace removal is confirmed before calling this endpoint as removal will be cascading all subsidiary elements within. With no cancellation service available.

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

409.description: There are objects in custom workspace that could not be removed

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

file.description:
Attach a resource file you need to use with your tasks.

To ensure that your tasks run smoothly without any security-related interruptions, it is recommended to encode the file in base64 and add a decoding step directly in the Jobs that require it.

This design not only helps protect your data but also avoids any potential security issues that may arise from Talend Cloud’s security mechanism interpreting the file.

get.description: Get Resource by id

put.description: Move a resource to another workspace. The target workspace must be in the same environment as the original one.

/orchestration/schedules.description:
Schedule are multiple scheduling items (Once, Daily, Weekly, Monthy, CRON) combination that can be prepared, tested and associated to one Task or Plan for triggering its executions.

Please be aware that : 
* This new Schedule API can apply to all types of Tasks or Plans, either new or existing ones
* The API allows to assist on the design of complex schedules, thanks to simulation of triggered events to come
* The API allows to prepare a schedule without taking risk to have it in action
* The API protects against redundant definition of triggers and too tight frequencies : the concurrency of triggers are checked up to 5 years, allowing bissextile years case to be taken into consideration
* The API protects against unwanted delete of schedules in action
* WebHooks triggers are **not part** of the Multiple Schedule since they are not scheduled triggers
* WebHooks are handled through the existing task and plan config endpoints
* Usages of WebHooks or Schedule are exclusive and can not be combined

get.description:
Retrieve schedules present on your account according to several optional search criteria :
* Environment
* Description
* Bound status : filter in or out orphan schedules or schedules in action on a task or plan

Please be aware user would need VIEW permission on the workspace where the schedule is in action and at least on one workspace within the environment for unlinked schedules.

post.description:
Create a new schedule providing one initial trigger item. 

You would be able to add some more triggers, update one trigger or remove it using the unique ressource endpoint for your created schedule. 

The created schedule is orphan, it means it is not related yet to any task or plan for your executions. 

User should be TMC_OPERATOR and have EXECUTOR Permission at least for one workspace in the environment.

Retrieve whole details of the schedule definition.

Please be aware user would need VIEW permission on the workspace when the schedule is in action on some task or plan.
When not in action, User should have VIEW permission at least on one workspace within the environment.

post.description:
Duplicate the current schedule : all the triggers present in the source schedule will be duplicated on the copy. 
If no name is provided for the schedule copy, some "copy of" source name will be given to target schedule copy. 
The newly created schedule wont be linked to any executable, but ready to be.

delete.description:
Delete the schedule object. Please note only orphan Schedules can be deleted. When the schedule is being used by an executable, It cannot be deleted. 
Therefore,  when you need to perform a schedule cleanup, unlink your schedule before removing it.

"Duplicate name or trigger definition" or "
New trigger causes too many concurrent events with current triggers in schedule"

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

post.summary: Append some trigger to your schedule
post.description:
Append some elementary trigger in the list. Some pre-conditions are checked : 
* the Trigger object must be valid
* Trigger name provided must be unique 
* you can add up to 15 elementary triggers in one schedule
* the Trigger can't be identical to one existing trigger : no duplicate allowed
* the new Trigger added should not bring new scheduled events that are concurrent to current events in the existing schedule : interval between two executions of the same task must not be less than 5 minutes. The concurrency of triggers are checked up to 5 years, allowing bissextile years case to be taken into consideration.

200.description: Trigger added to your Scheduler object

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

409.description:
"Duplicate name or trigger definition" or "
New trigger causes too many concurrent events with current triggers in schedule"

/orchestration/schedules/{scheduleId}/ev.description: Allow to compute next triggered events for a given date-time period provided in input

get.description:
Retrieve all the events that would be triggered by the schedule for a given date-time period. When no first datetime provided, the current datetime is taken as default value.
This endpoint is useful for checking the design of your schedule before putting it in use or when some changes is needed.  

Each triggered event is provided with the name of the elementary trigger that generated it : using this information you would be able to easily adapt the right trigger when needed. 

User should have VIEW permission on the workspace when the schedule is in action on some task or plan.
When not in action, User should have VIEW permission at least on one workspace within the environment.

get.description:
Get Task configuration currently applied. 
Please take note : 
* the Trigger definition can now have some new value 'Multiple' defined when you link your task to some Multiple Time Trigger schedule. 
* whenever such multiple trigger schedule is linked please use the new dedicated endpoints to manage this specific scheduling configuration.

put.description:
Configure Task regarding executions.
User must be granted `TMC_OPERATOR` permission
Timeout default value can be set as option : it would apply for terminating all executions of this task whose duration exceeds the time out provided in minutes. Accuracy is about 10s. Please note that this capability is available for CE, REC and RE version 2.12.2 and onwards.

/orchestration/executables/tasks/{taskId.description: Manage task configuration for Dynamic Engines.

get.summary: Get Task configuration for dynamic engine
get.description: Get Task configuration for dynamic engine currently applied.

put.summary: Configure Task executions for dynamic engine
put.description:
Configure how tasks are executed on Dynamic Engines.
* Permission required:  `TMC_OPERATOR`.
* Timeout option: Set a default timeout value (in minutes) to terminate task executions that exceed this duration. Note: Accuracy is within 10 seconds.

post.description: Get scheduled executions of all types

Search Tasks according to some filters:
* name
* environment id
* workspace id
* artifact id 
* runtime type
* runtime id
* runtime runprofile id
* tags
* trigger type

All those parameters are optional and can be combined at your convenience, apart from Environment and Workspace which are exclusive (ie you should not provide both in a same call)

put.description:
Allow to pause or resume a Task. 
When pausing a task all current executions will be completed, all new executions requests will be discarded, including scheduled, manual (through TMC or API) and webhook ones. 
When resuming a task all triggers (cron, webhook, etc.) will be reactivated, all new executions requests will be accepted again.

202.description: Successful response - Accepted (work is done in background)

403.description: Forbidden - no permissions to access resource

500.description: Server error - something went wrong on server side

EnvironmentInfo.description:
An Environment is some uniquely named space allowing you to organize your workspaces and subsidiary jobs definition within the cloud platform. 
Everything in TMC is related to an Environment : engines, workspaces, artifacts, tasks, plans, etc. 
Default preconisation is to define Environments according to your Software Process life cycle, for instance Development, QA, Pre-Production, Production. 
Workspace entity will allow you to organize your work within this Environment, according to your Business organization and security isolation rules. 
Of course, you can already choose to define Environments as subparts of your IT, for instance 'Production - Supply Chain', and choose the right grain of organization. 
Using Promotions, you would be able afterwards to define detailed rules for your software to be promoted across your Environments.

updateDate.description: Date of update of the workspace

ownerFullName.description: Full name of the workspace owner

protectedArtifactUpdate.description:
Allows to restrict the scope of task updates when a new artifact version is published. 
- true : the tasks of this workspace are protected from the unintended updates triggered by artifacts being published in other workspaces. These tasks are updated only when artifacts are being published in the same workspace
- false : tasks that have been set to use the latest version of the published artifact, or any other artifact that has the same name as the newly published artifact, are all updated, whatever workspace is used for this artifact publishing

note.description: Description to rerun a plan execution from this step

updateDate.description: Date of updating of the resource

version.description: Version of the current plan task

destination.description: Destination of the the current plan task

workspaceId.description: Id of the plan task's workspace

PlanPauseDetails.description: Contains status of Plan regarding Pause/Resume capacity, as well as optional contextual explanation for the pause. You can use this context for audit purpose to link information on the IT event that led to this pause (eg. maintenance window for prod operation).

PauseDetails.description: Contains status regarding Pause/Resume capacity, as well as optional contextual explanation for the pause. You can use this context to link information on the production event that led to this pause (eg. maintenance window for prod operation).

user.description: User that took the action to pause

pauseDate.description: The date time when this pause started

artifactType.description: Advanced Promotion Artifact Type. In the following list of allowed values, ‘ACTION’ refers to Artifacts, ‘FLOW’ refers to Tasks, ‘PLAN’ refers to Plans and ‘WORKSPACE’ refers to Workspaces

description.description: Promotion description (not returned when querying a list of objects)

workspaceId.description: Workspace id of task to create

targetVersion.description: Applicable to flows only, displays version of flow in target environment after promotion execution

version.description: Applicable to flows only, displays version of flow in source environment

userId.description: User who triggered or scheduled the execution

keepTargetRunProfiles.description: Keep Target Run Profiles flag (not returned for API versions earlier V2.2)

clusters.description: Remote Engine Clusters Promotion Execution Info

context.example: Free text describing the context for promotion execution

type.description: Defines the scope of the promotion execution

keepTargetRunProfiles.description: Dont touch run profiles on target

context.description: Optional context for audit log the promotion execution

file.description: Boolean value which indicates that resource is file

CreateResourceRequest.description: Resource detail : json payload with resource attributes

ScheduleTrigger.description:
Elementary Scheduling item. Also known as 'trigger', this schedule object can take different types : CRON, ONCE, DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY.     
    
Note that schedule updates that are made within three minutes prior to the scheduled execution time immediately impact the execution. For this reason, avoid making schedule changes when an execution is about to be triggered.

name.description: Name of your trigger, unique within a schedule

interval.description: Interval between task/plan running in days/weeks/months, required only if type of schedule is not equal to 'ONCE

startDate.description: Date when the schedule should start to be active

startTime.description: Time when the schedule should start to be active

times.description: Timestamps to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_SPECIFIC_TIMES

time.description: Timestamp to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_TIME

startTime.description: Interval start timestamp, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_INTERVALS

endTime.description: Interval end timestamp, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_INTERVALS

interval.description: Duration of interval in minutes, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_INTERVALS

day.description: Day of month to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at days is equal to DAY_OF_MONTH

days.description: List of days of week to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at days is equal to DAY_OF_WEEK

timeout.description: Timeout to apply (in minutes) to this trigger executions only. This value would override the default timeout possibly set on Task definition.

triggers.description: List of elementary triggers defined within schedule

environmentId.description: Id of the environment this schedule is part of

executableId.description: Id the task or plan this schedule is applied to. When empty, means the schedule is not in action.

executableType.description: Executable type, ie Task or Plan, to which this schedule applies to

CRON.description:
* CRON expression following  https://www.freeformatter.com/cron-expression-generator-quartz.html)
* seconds are not supported

triggerCalls.description: Endpoint call count to run task|plan

triggerTimeout.description: Time after which the task will starts from the moment the endpoint is first called

runAsUser.description: The user on behalf of whom the task will be launched

newUrl.description: Indicates whether to generate new url

executionTimeout.description: Time after which the task execution will be terminated on timeout

runAsUserType.description: The type of user on behalf of whom the task will be launched (HUMAN or SERVICE)

interval.description: Interval between task/plan running in days/weeks/months, required only if type of schedule is not equal to 'ONCE')

startDate.description: Date when the task should start to run

startTime.description: Time when the schedule should start to be active

times.description: Timestamps to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_SPECIFIC_TIMES

time.description: Timestamp to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_TIME

startTime.description: Interval start timestamp, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_INTERVALS

endTime.description: Interval end timestamp, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_INTERVALS

interval.description: Duration of interval in minutes, required only if type of schedule at times equal to AT_INTERVALS

connections.description: Task connections. Array of key-value pairs with string key that represents connection name and string value with connection id.

parameters.description: Task parameters. Array of key-value pairs with string key that represents parameter name and string value of parameter.

resources.description: Task resources. Array of key-value pairs with string key that represents resource name and string value with id of resource.

parallelExecutionAllowed.description: Indicates whether this executable is allowed to run in parallel. 'false' by default

timeout.description: Execution timeout in minutes. Minimal value is 1. Only available for CE and RE 2.12.2 and onwards.

deploymentStrategy.description: Deployment strategy for Route or Data Service jobs that run on Remote Engine Cluster

lineage.description:
Activates or deactivates the data lineage feature.

Data integration Jobs published from Talend Studio to Talend Management Console can generate input and output datasets and lineage information. This information can be sent to Qlik Cloud.

You can then view the data lineage, which tracks transformations back to the original source.

**Accepted values**:
- `true`
- `false`

workspaceId.description: Workspace id of task to create

connections.description: Task connections. Array of key-value pairs with string key that represents the connection's application name and string value with connection id.

parameters.description: Task parameters. Array of key-value pairs with string key that represents parameter name and string value. Value can accept both string and numeric data. Value is retrieved as string on GET task.

resources.description: Task resources. Array of key-value pairs with string key that represents resource name and string value with resource id.

environmentId.description: Environment id of task to create

latestArtifact.description: update task with latest artifact (Route and Data Service are not supported)

forceUpdate.description: force update task with latest artifact even if autoUpgradeInfo.autoUpgradable is false

name.description: Name of task (works as a "contains" filter)

runtimeType.description: Runtime type, valid runtime types are: REMOTE_ENGINE, PIPELINE_ENGINE, REMOTE_ENGINE_CLUSTER, CLOUD, CLOUD_EXCLUSIVE

runtimeRunProfileId.description: Runtime Run Profile ID, cannot be used without runtimeType filter

tags.description: Tags associated with tasks. When multiple tag values provided all those will be applied with logical AND

triggerType.description: Allow to filter according to trigger type applied to tasks

day.description: Day of month to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at days is equal to DAY_OF_MONTH

days.description: List of days of week to run task/plan, required only if type of schedule at days is equal to DAY_OF_WEEK

autoUpgradable.description: Indicates whether to automatically upgrade task after publishing new artifact version

overrideWithDefaultParameters.description: Indicates whether to override task parameters with artifact default parameters during auto upgrade

id.description: The entity's id for this event

sourceTriggerName.description: The source trigger's name (only for schedule for multiple triggers)

date.description: The Trigger's date for the event

type.description: The entity's type for the event, should be one of the following: "TASK", "PLAN"
Type of runtime, should be one of the following: "CLOUD", "REMOTE_ENGINE", "REMOTE_ENGINE_CLUSTER", "CLOUD_EXCLUSIVE", "PIPELINE_ENGINE"

workspaceIds.description: Workspace ids. If empty, will return events for all workspaces in the environment

from.description: From date time (milliseconds). Default value - now

limit.description: The number of items to return. Must be in range [1, 100]. Default value - 100

offset.description: The position of the first item. Must be greater than or equal to 0. Default value - 0

TaskPauseDetails.description: Holds the definition of a Task Pause / Resume

user.description: User that took the action to pause

pauseDate.description: Date time when this pause started

DETaskRunConfig.description: Task run configuration used for dynamic engine processor

parallelExecutionAllowed.description: Indicates whether this executable is allowed to run in parallel. 'false' by default

timeout.description: Execution timeout in minutes. Minimal value is 1.

runProfileId.description: The dynamic engine run profile id

lineage.description:
Activates or deactivates the data lineage feature.

Data integration Jobs published from Talend Studio to Talend Management Console can generate input and output datasets and lineage information. This information can be sent to Qlik Cloud.

You can then view the data lineage, which tracks transformations back to the original source.

**Accepted values**:
- `true`
- `false`

id.description:
Target runtime id, either Remote engine id or Remote engine cluster id
Empty for cloud engines

engineId.description: Remote engine id used for execution

clusterId.description: Remote engine cluster id used for execution

runProfileId.description: Run profile id of Remote engine or Remote engine cluster

detail.example: Token authorization is required for access

requestId.description: The unique identification of the request involved with this error

Public.description: This Bearer authentication can be set in the Authorization header of your requests. Authentication tokens and Personal Access Tokens are supported. Personal Access Tokens can be generated in the Profile Preferences page of the Talend Cloud Portal.
